channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Author Correction: Operando microimaging of crystal structure and orientation in all components of all-solid-state-batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69145-1

Author Correction: Operando microimaging of crystal structure and orientation in all components of all-solid-state-batteries
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Operando microimaging of crystal structure and orientation in all components of all-solid-state-batteries
item.dc:creator: Quentin Jacquet
Jacopo Cele
Lara Casiez
Samuel Tardif
Asma Medjahed
Stephanie Pouget
Manfred Burghammer
Sandrine Lyonnard
Sami Oukassi

item.title: Hierarchical picot-fiber hydrogel coating with ultralow friction and high wear resistance
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69322-2

Hydrogel coatings have potential in friction reduction, but achieving the required durability is challenging. Here, the authors report a picot fibre hydrogel coating with a hierarchical structure, including a lubricious surface layer and fibre-reinforced core layer.
item.dc:title: Hierarchical picot-fiber hydrogel coating with ultralow friction and high wear resistance
item.dc:creator: Wei Sun
Xiaoxiao Sun
Junsheng Zhang
Lin Wu
Juan Wang
Wei Wang
Yi Cao
Bin Xue

item.title: Tough transparent glass ceramics for muti-mode programmable dynamic tunable persistent luminescence via phase engineering
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69202-9

Realizing tunable persistent luminescence in a single solid is promising for applications, but challenging. Here, the authors report tough transparent glass ceramics for muti-mode programmable dynamic tunable persistent luminescence via phase engineering.
item.dc:title: Tough transparent glass ceramics for muti-mode programmable dynamic tunable persistent luminescence via phase engineering
item.dc:creator: Yixi Wu
Xinkuo Li
Chao Ruan
Ke Sun
Jianrong Qiu
Dezhi Tan

item.title: Topological mechanical metamaterial for robust and ductile one-way fracturing
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69026-7

An architected structural material can steer cracks in a single direction, turning brittle fractures into predictable and ductile processes through topological mechanics, offering a robust route to programmable, safer failure across materials and scales.
item.dc:title: Topological mechanical metamaterial for robust and ductile one-way fracturing
item.dc:creator: Xinyu Wang
Siddhartha Sarkar
Stefano Gonella
Xiaoming Mao
